questions.question: The wildcard characters of the percent sign and the underscore (%, _) are used to specify pattern in a column for the operator ______.
Which of the following is NOT an ANSI standard command?
A SQL function that allows the user to access a specific set of records from a database table is called a(n) SQL _______.
Which of the following conditions will NOT check if field1 has a value of "OFF"?
options: SELECT * FROM table WHERE NOT field1 != "OFF"
SELECT * FROM table WHERE field1 IN ("OFF")
SELECT * FROM table WHERE field1 >= "OFF" AND field1 <= "OFF"

questions.question: A full outer join between table1 and table2 is equivalent to _________.
options: FULL OUTER join is equivalent to a CROSS join between the two tables
All Records from table1 and table2 that they don’t share
Only data that matches in both tables
All data that matches in both tables, or exists in either table

questions.question: Which statement would get the records where field1 has a value between 100 and 200 (not including 100 and 200)?
A LIMIT clause is important because it improves efficiency by restricting how many records the database has to access.
The order of operations for logical operators in SQL is
The query SELECT 1885 & 170 OR TRUE?
Which of the following is a valid data type?
Why would this query fail - CREATE TABLE table1 (int personID, char yesNo)
options: Char is not a valid data type.
Datatype and field name not in the correct order

questions.question: Which Query will NOT find all rows where the character field, field1 is 'a'?
options: SELECT * FROM table1 WHERE field1 like 'a';
SELECT * FROM table1 WHERE field1 in (SELECT 'a')

questions.question: Which is NOT an advantage of a normalized database?
options: Reduces the average number of joins to access data

questions.question: To list the number of tables in a database you would use the ____ command.
What would be the outcome of the following SELECT statement - SELECT field1, "* ", field2 FROM table;
options: All the values in field1, followed by all the values from each field in the table, followed by the values in field2
All the values from each field in the table with first1 as the first column and field2 as the last column
All the values in each field in the table, excluding field1 and field 2
All the values in field1, *, all the values in field2

questions.question: Database normalization rules were established in what decade?
Which of the following statements is true about a UNION ALL statement?
options: The resulting data will come out sorted
The resulting data will be free from duplicates
UNION ALL is faster than UNION
UNION ALL can take a list of tables to combine

questions.question: The acronym that stands for the process of manipulating data using insert, update, and delete is _____
A Primary Key consisting of multiple fields is known as a ____.
How many normalization forms are there?
Which normalization for states that an entity should depend solely on the unique identifier of the entity?
When running a query, the aggregate function COUNT(field1) includes entries where field1 is NULL.
The transaction property stating a transaction will either be performed in its entirety or not at all is known as ____.
The acronym for the standard properties of a transaction is ____.
An intersection is the result of what type of join?
Which WHERE clause would check if the field1 contains "appl ", "pear", or "grape"
options: WHERE field1 = "apple" AND field1 = "pear" AND field1 = "grape"

questions.question: An SQL Trigger is used to ____.
options: Speed up the time it takes to run a query
Manage who has access to the database

questions.question: An Autoincrement field in SQL ____.
options: Assigns random numbers to a key field of a table
Is required if a table does not have a primary key
Puts a table in 2nd normal form

questions.question: How many INSERT queries would be necessary to save “Tony” was in the movie “Tiny”
What level of normalization is table Movie?
options: It does not pass any normalization forms

questions.question:
What would be the order of the actorId given the following query: 
SELECT DISTINCT c.actorID FROM Movie a
INNER JOIN MovieActor b ON a.movieId = b.movieId
INNER JOIN Actor c ON b.actorId = c. actorId
WHERE genreId = 1 ORDER by name DESC, dob ASC
Which JOIN & WHERE clause would produce Actors who were not in any movies? SELECT name FROM actor a
options: LEFT JOIN MovieActor b ON a.actorId = b.actorId WHERE b.movieId IS NULL
INNER JOIN MovieActor b ON a.actorID = b.actorId WHERE b.movieID IS NULL
RIGHT JOIN MovieActor b ON a.actorID = b.actorId WHERE b.movieID IS NULL
Can’t join if there are no actors to join to.

questions.question: Which query would produce the youngest actor / actress and the movie they were in? SELECT title, name, dob FROM Movie
options: INNER JOIN Actor b ON a.movieId = b.movieId WHERE dob = MAX(dob)
INNER JOIN MovieActor b ON a.movieId = b.movieId INNER JOIN Actor c ON b.actorId = c.actorId WHERE dob = MAX(dob)
INNER JOIN MovieActor b ON a.movieId = b.movieId INNER JOIN Actor c ON b.actorId = c.actorId WHERE dob in (SELECT MAX(dob) FROM Actor)
INNER JOIN MovieActor b ON a.movieId = b.movieId INNER JOIN Actor c ON b.actorId = c.actorId WHERE dob = (NOW()-dob)

questions.question: What is the highest level of normalization in SQL?
After reviewing the data, it was noticed that Jane was also in the movie Shape. What statement would add her to the Movie table.
What does the acronym DCL stand for?
What is the difference between TRUNCATE and DELETE in SQL
options: DELETE is a DCL command, TRUNCATE is a DML command
TRUNCATE data is recoverable, DELETE is not
TRUNCATE removes schema, table, constraints, and triggers; Delete removes just the data
DELETE can be used with a WHERE clause, TRUNCATE can not.

questions.question: Referential Integrity refers to ____.
options: The consistency of the Super Key in relation to the Primary Key
The accuracy and consistency of data within a relationship
Maintaining foreign keys between two tables

questions.question: A nonrelational database ____.
options: Stores data in a non-tabular format
Is traditionally used for smaller data stores
Is best in non-cloud related applications.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a main relationship type in relational database design?
A data modeling technique that graphically illustrates information and relationships within a database is a(n) ____.
Cardinality of a relationship in database terms refers to the number of ____ it contains.
Which of the following is NOT an Index type in SQL?
Functions / methods that are saved in a database are called?
The result of a CROSS JOIN is known as a _____.
Using a varchar(150) data field, how many characters would “Business Professional of America” typcially take up?
How would you empty the table “Business”, but leave the structure of the table intact?
How would you add a foreign key on other_Id from a table named other to a field main_Id in a related table named main?
options: UPDATE other SET FOREIGN KEY ON other_Id = main.main_Id
MODIFY other ADD FOREIGN KEY ON other_Id = main.main_Id
ALTER TABLE other ADD FOREIGN KEY other ON other_Id = main.main_Id
ALTER TABLE other ADD CONSTRAINT otherToMain FOREIGN KEY (other_id) REFERENCES main (main_id)

questions.question: The purpose of a rollback in SQL is to ____.
options: To undelete data when using – DELETE ALL FROM table
To undo everything that was done in a Transaction
To Allow a DBA to undo database updates that have been committed

questions.question: A disadvantage of OLAP would be its ____.
